title.value: Palladium-Catalyzed Intermolecular [5 + 2] Cycloaddition: Highly Regioselective Synthesis of Allene-Containing 2-Benzoxepines

abstract.value: Palladium-catalyzed intermolecular [5 + 2] cycloaddition of propargylic carbonates with benzyl allenyl ethers has been developed for the synthesis of allene-containing 2-benzoxepines. The reaction proceeds by a pathway involving decarboxylative allenylpalladium formation, regioselective intermolecular insertion, and intramolecular nucleophilic attack. In this way, the sequential formation of C-C and C-O bonds results in the generation of a 2-benzoxepine skeleton in a one-pot process.
